name: Process uploaded files with Google Drive and Notion end to end

parameters.text: =✅ Process uploaded files with Google Drive and Notion end to end — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 6 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: List Changed Docs (Google Drive)

### List Changed Docs (Google Drive)

googleDrive — `fileFolder` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Remove Duplicate Documents · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Remove Duplicate Documents

Drops duplicates within this run (compared on `documentId`).

Next: Download File Content

### Download File Content

googleDrive — `download` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Extract Text from File · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Extract Text from File

Parses the incoming PDF binary into JSON items.

Next: Attach Chunk Metadata

### Attach Chunk Metadata

Custom JavaScript: Attach chunk-level metadata before embedding

Next: Insert into Vector Store

### Insert into Vector Store

Writes embedded chunks into the vector index.

Ports: ①→ Count Indexed Chunks · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Qdrant URL + key

### Prepare Documents

Wraps incoming items as documents (with metadata) for the vector store.

Feeds **Insert into Vector Store** as `document`

### Chunk with Overlap

Chunks documents (~1200 chars, 120 overlap) so retrieval hits stay focused.

Feeds **Prepare Documents** as `textSplitter`

### Document Embeddings

Embedding model — MUST be the same model for indexing and querying, or retrieval quality collapses.

Feeds **Insert into Vector Store** as `embedding`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Count Indexed Chunks

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per document (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Copy to Google Drive

### Archive Copy to Google Drive

googleDrive — `upload` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Summarize Run Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Archive Copy to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

# Process uploaded files with Google Drive and Notion end to end

**Pattern:** File Processing · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Process uploaded files with Google Drive and Notion end to end. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual summarization is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → List Changed Docs (Google Drive) → Remove Duplicate Documents → Download File Content → Extract Text from File → Attach Chunk Metadata → Insert into Vector Store → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to file processing without manual effort
- Anyone automating summarization in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google AI API key; Google OAuth2; Notion integration token; Qdrant URL + key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your summarization process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
